description: Porygon must learn Tri Attack (TM49) — Gen 1 exclusive TM, not available in any later gen
Eevee — one-time gift from Celadon Mansion rooftop. Only one per save.
Omanyte — revive Helix Fossil at Cinnabar Lab. One fossil choice per save (Omanyte OR Kabuto).
Kabuto — revive Dome Fossil at Cinnabar Lab. One fossil choice per save (Omanyte OR Kabuto).
Aerodactyl — revive Old Amber at Cinnabar Lab. One per save.
Hitmonlee — one-time gift at Fighting Dojo. Choose Hitmonlee OR Hitmonchan.
Hitmonchan — one-time gift at Fighting Dojo. Choose Hitmonlee OR Hitmonchan.
Lapras — one-time gift in Silph Co. after defeating rival. One per save.
Snorlax — two static encounters (Route 12 and Route 16). Requires Poké Flute.
Articuno — static encounter in Seafoam Islands. One per save.
Zapdos — static encounter at Power Plant. One per save.
Moltres — static encounter at Victory Road. One per save.
Mewtwo — static encounter in Cerulean Cave (post-game). One per save.
Bulbasaur — one-time gift from girl in Cerulean City (Yellow only, requires Pikachu happiness).
Charmander — one-time gift from man on Route 24 (Yellow only).
Squirtle — one-time gift from Officer Jenny in Vermilion City (Yellow only).
Pikachu — starter in Yellow. One per save, cannot be released.
Mr. Mime — in-game trade for Abra on Route 2 (only way to get Mr. Mime in Gen 1 without link trade).
Lickitung — Red/Blue only via Safari Zone. Not available in Yellow.
Jynx — in-game trade for Poliwhirl in Cerulean City (Red/Blue). In Yellow, obtainable in Seafoam Islands.
Tauros — Safari Zone only. Low encounter rate, worth catching multiple for trade fodder.
Nidorina can learn TM moves in Gen 1 that she can't in later gens. Teach before evolving/transferring.
